Okay, so welcome back in this lesson. We are going to give a full exploration of exactly what we're talking about when we're saying unpacking the situational gestalt and why you may want to consider using it as your framework on the first part of your sales cause. So fair warning, this is going to be slightly advanced as we explain it, but it's one of those things that once you get, you get. In your mind may crave greater control, you know, in the form of some word by word script or perfectly crafted sequences or structured questions. But I want you to remember that we're dropping this whole lie of linearity in favor of a more natural approach that actually aligns with and respects the more instantaneous quantum exponential reality of a sales conversion. So things happen in a more exponential way, a more quantum way, not necessarily in this linear fashion. And this framework is what actually gives us a chance to honor that and respect it in the way that we allow our conversations to flow. So there are really three main universal laws in a situational gestalt. So it's based off three core ideas. Number one, that no emotion or feeling exists in a silo that they always actually include their polarity. We're going to talk more about that throughout this lesson. Also sales is alchemical. You don't overcome a feeling. You don't get them over any idea. You don't blast past resistance. You don't ignore or bypass the past. You actually transform it into something more favorable. And then finally, evolution is integrative meaning to go forward. It must actually include the past, right? We don't sever that. We don't ignore it. We don't pretend it doesn't exist. We don't stay only future focused, right? Evolution integrates everything that's happened before that point in time. So let's go deeper into the first law. No emotion or feeling exists in a silo and they always include their polarity. So what are we talking about here? So many of us have heard we make decisions with our emotions and justify it with our logic. And that is partially true at least. You know, the purchase decision is indeed mostly emotionally driven, but it's often not just a single emotion like desire or hopefulness or excitement or fear. So it's not an isolated emotion, but an entire gestalt representing the totality of their inner experience around the problem, the goal or the situation. So what is a gestalt? A gestalt has two or more objects or emotions, beliefs, memories, thought patterns that are so woven and integrated together that we simply perceive them as one object. So what are examples of a situational gestalt? Well, a freelance career that is a makeup of so many objects of hope, streams, fears, memories, struggles, all of that is included in this goal or this situation of a freelance career. So your attempt to scale from 50 grand a year to 100,000, same with your goal of playing the guitar. There's so much woven into that goal, into that situation, your feelings around sales, your fitness, your weight loss journey, your desire for greater connection with your kids or your partner. All these situations have so many objects within them that are so tightly bound together and woven together that it looks like one thing, but there's actually far more that has to be unpacked within it. So there is no such thing as an isolated emotion or experience or goal. So all of these goal or desire or situational gestalts are essentially just title holders for a far wider spectrum of emotion, including that emotion's reverse pull. Or another way of saying it is that for every positive or empowering view, belief or emotion we have about a topic, we also carry at least to some degree its negative or more disempowering charge. So here's the key thing, we also tend to view our future far more optimistically and favorably than our pasts. Meaning most of the negative charge of the poll exists as past evidence, right? Things that actually happen, it's data, it actually happened in our past while the positive poll is more ephemeral and flimsy and based in the future that has yet to be experienced. So if you take a look at this full situational gestalt, right, where we have the future on one column and the past on another, you can see desire is mostly future focused. But it does include the past, which is longing, right, of not having that desire fulfilled, longing for that desire, lack of that desire, the pain of not having it, the envy of others who have achieved that desire and even the sense of unfairness and injustice that one might have that others have achieved that and I haven't, right? And if you go down the list, something like anticipation around the future, so positive anticipation of essentially implicating yourself in this and going after that desire, well that might also include the past that carries a certain level of anxiety, right? Of urgency of, oh my goodness, I have to do this, right? That's a negative poll of anticipation and the fear of failure. What if I try? What if I move forward and things don't go according to plan? How will I actually feel about myself? Next, the future can carry some degree of expansion, right? And a renewed possibility of the outcome happening or manifesting or whatever you want to call it. However, that also includes the negative poll mostly rooted in the past that could include contraction, right? Doubt, hopelessness, despair of all these things not happening. In the future, what you're trying to create is a sense of renewed confidence in themselves and their abilities, but it must include the past, right? It must include frustration, failure, struggle, guilt, shame, all the things that they have felt that are on the negative poll of confidence. Same, clarity includes confusion and overwhelm. Readiness includes moments of remorse of having not gone started earlier, right? Remorse of how the problem may have metastasized into something even worse. Regret, resistance, towards moving forward. All that is included within readiness and rightness. That also includes wrongness in the path or the process that they may have experienced before. So ultimately, what you want is you want to move them as much as possible throughout the call into that positive side, right? Where the result of all seven of those things will be courage and the power to move forward and the result of having put that entire past, that whole charged negative experience on the table, the result there will be a sense of catharsis, a sense of relief, right? That they're no longer carrying all that stuff and relief that despite that entire past, the future, what they're evolving towards has actually taken it all into account. And this is what's also going to create a sense of safety and moving forward. So all of this is included together. And you could see this in how one might explain their goal or their desire for freelance copyright and career, right? So I might say I really want this to work, which is both desire and longing. As I hate my corporate agency job in the long hours they make us work for no extra pay, there's your frustration and struggle. I want to have that flexibility and smash the income ceiling that others in this field have. So in that, you see desire, you see envy, you see longing, you see injustice, but have had trouble knowing the fastest path to making this work. Wrongness, confusion and overwhelm. I've spent every spare hour I've had listening to podcasts and reading posts on copyhackers, but I'm at the post point where I'm running out of time. That's a sense of failure and I need to put it all together fast. So there's their sense of urgency. So you can see how even in just what, you know, this might represent 45 seconds of dialogue, there are so many elements of this table present. And this is what makes these calls nonlinear, right? We can't talk about desire without also talking about longing and injustice and envy and pain. We can't talk about anticipating a better future without also talking about the urgency and the fear of failure around it. So all this stuff comes together in what we call a situational gestalt. So an exercise I would invite you to embark on is to simply write out the situational gestalt that most mirrors the last perfect fit call you've had, where everything seemed to have been put up on the table and things seemed to flow. Then see if you can identify the emotional elements of the gestalt and notice how both the positive and negative are often mentioned in the same sentence and sometimes even reflected in the same words like in this example of the freelance copywriter. So why are we doing this? Well, the goal of unpacking the situational gestalt is to get your prospect feeling emptied of every charged element around that experience because all gestalt ultimately require resolution and closure. And the first step in doing so is having the fullness of them revealed because once they're on the table in the presence of safety, power and expanded vision, you are now able to transform them, which brings us to universal law number two, sales is our chemical we don't seek to overcome these individual elements, we seek to transform them. So the polarities, which we spoke about all exist on a spectrum. And the goal is to get each one to 51% on the positive side to essentially tip the scales into an empowered conversion. So we're not looking to get 100% desire, we're simply trying to get all the longing, lack pain, envy and injustice on the table, then moving someone into a greater focus and weightiness on their desire, which they're now free to have more of once the past has been unpacked. Same thing, we're not trying to get pure anticipation to the exclusion of any urgency, anxiety or fear of failure, we're simply seeking to tip the scale. So this is all about tipping the scales to the more positive side of that polarity in which the result will be a sense of empowerment instead of disempowerment. So once again, we don't seek to overcome all limiting thoughts, emotions and viewpoints, but transform them just enough to tip the scales in the direction of their positive pull. So this is what makes their past experience and negative charge embraced by their current vision and included within that vision, rather than bypassed, ignored or even shamed. An empowered client will ultimately feel more on that right side than the left, but not because the left is bypass suppressed or made unconscious, but because it's been revealed, released and given a chance to be redeemed and re-channeled into their vision and desire. Once we're only seeking for each stage, for each of those seven aspects to be more tipped to the positive side, we don't actually need to unpack elements of the Gestalt where they're already empowered. This is why these calls can be anywhere between 10 minutes to 30 minutes in this section. If they're already expressing desire and readiness without the tones, without the experience of remorse, regret and being injustice, we don't need to probe and dig for something that might not actually be there. This is another major mistake people make on sales calls. They are looking to agitate wounds that don't need to be agitated when someone is already on the positive side of that pull. So remember, they all exist in spectrum or a dial of 1 to 100. It's possible your pre-customer is already more clear than unclear, more ready than resistant and you don't have to spend a second talking about the negative pull of that aspect. Because if we're not careful, our conversations can also be of disservice and tip someone from the positive to the negative charge. This is why linear scripts that demand that you probe for fears and probe for consequences can actually have the counter effect if someone is already ready. So if I'm empowered and excited and ready to get relationship and intimacy coaching, I can actually be dissuaded if the coach relentlessly minds for all these imagined exaggerated consequences of an action because he's actually relating to an element of the Gestalt resistance that I'm already clear of. He's actually layering and imposing it onto my experience and tilting me onto the negative side of the pull. So notice, nowhere does it say we need to excavate fears. A sale can be made without ever giving voice to current pain. That alone imagined projected, exaggerated pain or consequences. If one is already empowered in their healthy desire, then once again I'll repeat drilling for and insisting on fear is actually more harmful and detrimental to the sale. Likewise, if they're already in greater courage than fear, then focusing on that fear is detrimental. It just slides them down that scale. So the bottom line is don't search for fear or any negative elements of the pull that really isn't present. And don't search for confusion, don't search for overwhelm or anxiety, or greater levels of clarity and anticipation already exist. Otherwise, you'll risk moving them down towards states of disempowerment. Universal law number three evolution is integrative meaning. It includes the past. So your goal in the first 20 to 30 minutes of this call is for your preclin to feel total relief and catharsis around the baggage of the past and the belief that the present and future is inclusive of it. So evolution is inclusive. It's not abandoning. It's integrative. It's redeeming. And by involving the past in the future, they feel safe embodying and acting on the virtues, qualities, states and emotions on the left side of that chart. These right over here. Therefore, in summary, an enrollment conversation will hold both positive and negative poles of the situational gestalt, both the past and both the future simultaneously. It will cover as much of the totality of one's experience on the topic and it's ultimately a movement towards evolution and progress all while being inclusive and integrative of the past. So once again, the goal is even if after 10 minutes of talking to a prospect, you're 100% certain your solution is perfect for them. This is your opportunity, your duty to put your excitement and certainty aside. Don't do them or yourself the disservice of proposing the prescription until they fully put all their symptoms and fears on the table and felt a degree of catharsis around the emotions behind them because the offer, the prognosis, the prescription will only be as strong and resonant as your prospects belief conviction that it's taken the totality of their past and that entire gestalt into account. So once again, catharsis proceeds courage and you can't transmute. You can't transform what hasn't been put on the table, nor can you redeem or reintegrate a moment or past history that hasn't been allowed to resurface and you certainly can't offer relief on an emotion, belief or false destiny that hasn't been revealed. So that's the goal. Do not propose a solution or offer an invitation until you've revealed and unpacked as much as is necessary from this whole situational gestalt and shifted each element to its positive future focused pull. So that is the invitation that is a lot of information. You may want to rewatch this a few times, but the rest of this module will be dedicated to breaking down the nuances and the skills that we use within unpacking the gestalt. So take a breather, take a moment and when you're ready, meet me in the next lesson. Bye for now.
